narration: Here's a math fact that'll change every purchase you make. Every dollar you spend in your thirties is secretly worth seven dollars in retirement.
visual: Dark background. Large dollar coin with amber pulse rings. Arrow pointing to a 7x coin. Amber badge springs in: '$1 TODAY = $7.61 LATER · 7% returns · 30 years'.

narration: Seven percent returns over thirty years turns one dollar into seven sixty-one. That's compound interest always running silently in the background.
visual: Light background. Animated bar chart — bars grow from left to right showing $1 at year 0 growing to $7.61 at year 30, with milestone labels at years 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30.

narration: Number one — that two-hundred-dollar dinner. Your retirement account sees that as fourteen hundred dollars walking out the door, not two hundred.
visual: Dark background. Fork-and-knife SVG icon. $200 price tag slides in. Times-7 arrow. Red $1,400 badge springs in labeled 'RETIREMENT COST'.

narration: Number two — Americans spend almost fifteen hundred dollars every month on non-essentials. In retirement value that's over a hundred twenty-five thousand dollars a year your future self won't have.
visual: Light background. List of expense rows animate in — Dining $340, Shopping $280, Entertainment $220, Subscriptions $265, Impulse Buys $392. Red total badge: '$125,748/YEAR IN RETIREMENT VALUE'.

narration: Number three — millionaires don't spend less because they're cheap. They ask one question before every purchase: is this worth seven times the price to future me?
visual: Dark background. Two person silhouettes — gray spender with money flying away, amber saver with growing piggy bank. Question bubble at bottom: 'IS THIS WORTH 7× TO FUTURE ME?'.

narration: Before your next big purchase, multiply the price by seven. If it's still worth it, buy it guilt-free. If not, your future self just got a lot richer.
visual: Light background. Calculator SVG with × 7 display counting up. Amber CTA badge: 'STILL WORTH IT? BUY IT. / IF NOT — FUTURE YOU WINS.'
